title: Double Agent
shortDescription: Simultaneous Insider Threat on Two Competing Firms

briefing.incidentSummary:
Two rival fintech firms — PayAxis and ClearVault — both reported simultaneous data breaches on the same night.

PayAxis lost its fraud detection model weights. ClearVault lost its transaction risk scoring engine.

When investigators compared the breach signatures, something impossible emerged: both attacks were executed with the same tools, same timing pattern, and routed through the same intermediate server.

The perpetrator had active employment at both companies — simultaneously.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Establish that both breaches share a single operator

› Identify the employee working at both firms under different identities

› Understand what data was taken and where it went

› Collect at least 4 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Ananya V
suspects.role: Senior ML Engineer — PayAxis / 'Anya Verma' — ClearVault

digitalFootprint.locationTrace: Mumbai — same residential IP for both breach sessions
digitalFootprint.vpnCheck: Different VPNs used per company — both traced to same physical location

suspects.profileNotes: Employed at PayAxis as Ananya V (ML Engineer) and simultaneously at ClearVault as 'Anya Verma' (Data Scientist) — same person, different PAN card (one forged). Exfiltrated both firms' proprietary ML models on the same night, routing everything through a shared relay server she controlled. Sold both datasets to a Singapore-based hedge fund for $1.1 million total.

suspects.name: Sajan T
suspects.profileNotes: Failed to run a cross-company employment check during onboarding. The forged PAN used to join PayAxis went unverified. Negligent but not complicit.

suspects.name: Rahil D
suspects.profileNotes: Sat adjacent to 'Anya Verma' at ClearVault. No unusual access patterns. Became suspicious of her after she asked unusual questions about model checkpoint file paths.

suspects.name: Unknown Buyer — Meridian Capital
suspects.profileNotes: Received both datasets — is the demand side of the operation. However the technical act of exfiltration was entirely Ananya V's. The buyer is a separate investigation.

items.label: Both packages received. Initiating transfer — $550K per dataset as agreed.
items.detail: Recovered from an encrypted messaging app on a device linked to Ananya V's home IP. Confirms simultaneous delivery of both companies' datasets to a single buyer — one seller, two victims.

items.label: She kept asking me where we store model checkpoints. Said it was for a personal project. Felt weird.
items.detail: Rahil D raised a concern to ClearVault's security team 3 days before the breach. The alert was logged but not acted on in time.

items.label: BOTH breach sessions routed via relay 45.77.190.22. Timestamp delta: 4 minutes.
items.detail: Cross-company network forensics finding. PayAxis breach started at 2:11 AM, ClearVault breach at 2:15 AM — same relay IP, 4 minutes apart. Statistically impossible coincidence. One operator ran both sessions simultaneously.

items.label: dual_employment_verification.pdf
items.detail: Cross-referencing PayAxis and ClearVault employee records: photo comparison, biometric data from onboarding, and mobile number triangulation confirm Ananya V (PayAxis) and Anya Verma (ClearVault) are the same person. The PAN used at ClearVault is forged — digitally altered from her real PAN.

items.label: payaxis_data_exfil_log.txt
items.detail: PayAxis SIEM: 8.2GB outbound transfer from PAYAXIS-ML-03 at 2:11 AM via HTTPS to relay 45.77.190.22. Machine assigned to Ananya V. Data fingerprint matches fraud detection model checkpoint files.

items.label: clearvault_data_exfil_log.txt
items.detail: ClearVault SIEM: 6.7GB outbound transfer from CLEAR-ENG-07 at 2:15 AM via HTTPS to the same relay 45.77.190.22. Machine assigned to Anya Verma. Data fingerprint matches risk scoring engine model files.

hiddenItem.label: relay_server_ownership.txt
hiddenItem.detail: Relay server 45.77.190.22 is a VPS registered to an email address ananya.work.ops@proton.me — traced via payment records to Ananya V's personal credit card. She owned and operated the relay herself. This was not a commercial VPN — it was a custom exfiltration node she controlled.

minigame.title: Decode Relay Server Owner
minigame.hint: Caesar shift -3. Who owned the relay server?
hints: It is the real name behind both identities — followed by their initial.

onSuccess.message: relay_server_ownership.txt is now available in your file feed.

items.label: Shared Residential IP
items.detail: Both PAYAXIS-ML-03 and CLEAR-ENG-07 were VPN-connected during the breach. VPN exit nodes differ — but both VPN accounts were activated from the same residential IP: 122.178.44.90. That IP is registered to Ananya V's home broadband in Bandra, Mumbai.

items.label: Exfiltration Tool Signature
items.detail: Both breaches used the same custom Python exfiltration script — identical function names, identical chunk size (512KB), identical retry logic. The script is not publicly available. Same author wrote both — or it is literally the same script run twice.

items.label: Employment Date Overlap
items.detail: Ananya V joined PayAxis 14 months ago. 'Anya Verma' joined ClearVault 11 months ago — 3 months into her PayAxis tenure. Both employment offers accepted on the same day. HR at neither company checked cross-employment clauses.

items.label: Shared Relay Server
items.detail: Both breach data streams passed through 45.77.190.22 before reaching the buyer's endpoint. The relay is a custom VPS — not a commercial service. Owned by Ananya V. Using one relay for both breaches simultaneously is what made the connection detectable.

timeline.title: Joins PayAxis
timeline.description: Ananya V hired as Senior ML Engineer

timeline.title: Buyer Contact Established
timeline.description: Meridian Capital approaches via encrypted channel

timeline.title: Colleague Raises Flag
timeline.description: Rahil D reports unusual questions about model checkpoints — not acted on

timeline.title: PayAxis Exfiltration
timeline.description: 8.2GB fraud detection model transferred via relay

timeline.title: ClearVault Exfiltration
timeline.description: 6.7GB risk scoring engine transferred via same relay

timeline.title: Both Companies Raise Alarms
timeline.description: SIEM alerts fire — joint investigation begins

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Ananya V exposed as the double agent behind both breaches.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

partial.title: Insufficient Evidence
partial.subtitle: Correct suspect but the chain is incomplete.
partial.label: PARTIAL

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Ananya V.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

aria.welcomeToHub:
...Two companies. Same night. Same relay. Same script.
This wasn't two attackers. This was one person — in two places at once.
Find the person behind both names.
aria.exploreFeeds:
Employment records. Dual SIEM logs. Relay traces. Script signatures.
The impossibility is the clue. One relay used for two simultaneous breaches.
aria.viewEvidence:
Same home IP. Same exfil script. Same relay. 4 minutes apart.
This wasn't coordination between two people. This was one.
aria.markEvidence: Good. The double identity is unraveling.
aria.decryptionHint:
The relay server is registered to a name.
Decode who owns it.
aria.flagSuspect:
A trace is forming.
You're on to something — keep pulling the thread.
More fragments remain. Don't stop now.
